fields.description:
## Why

`.github/workflows/reconcile-bridge.yml` already has the full key-materialize + job-level
signing wiring (story 9e76/cc0b): it reads the `REBAR_BOT_SIGNING_KEY` secret, masks it
(`::add-mask::`), writes it to a `0600` file at `${RUNNER_TEMP}/rebar-bot-signing-key`, and
exports `REBAR_IDENTITY_SIGNING_KEY=<that path>` into `$GITHUB_ENV` (see
`.github/workflows/reconcile-bridge.yml:217-228`), with the bot email defaulted to
`joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com` (`:205`, `:267`) so the reconciler-authored events resolve to
identity `594c-9dcf-5ad6-4e6d`. The wiring is **secret-gated**: if `REBAR_BOT_SIGNING_KEY` is
empty the materialize step logs "writes will be unsigned (advisory)" and exits 0 (`:220-222`),
so CI writes come out **attributed but unsigned**. This is provisioning + verification of that
existing wiring, not new plumbing.

## Precondition — identity + wiring already exist (verified, not created)

- The **Rebar Bot identity** `594c-9dcf-5ad6-4e6d` already exists (`email
  joeoakhart+bot@navapbc.com`, one registered ed25519 key) — the same identity local signing
  (79da) uses; CI resolves to it by the `BRIDGE_BOT_EMAIL` default.
- The **private key** is available from AWS SSM SecureString `/rebar/prod/rebar-bot-signing-key`
  (ADR-0012) — the public half of which is the key registered on 594c.
- The **GitHub secret `REBAR_BOT_SIGNING_KEY` is already present** (`gh secret list` shows it,
  last set 2026-07-14). Because a GitHub secret value cannot be read back, this story
  **re-sets it from the authoritative SSM source** (to guarantee it is the correct current key)
  and then **proves** signing by triggering a real run — the secret's mere presence is not
  proof it holds the right key.

## What

Turn on real CI signing and prove it end-to-end:

- **Re-set** the `REBAR_BOT_SIGNING_KEY` GitHub Actions secret on `navapbc/rebar` from the SSM
  private key (`gh secret set REBAR_BOT_SIGNING_KEY --repo navapbc/rebar` fed the decrypted SSM
  value; never echoed).
- **Trigger** a `reconcile-bridge` run (`workflow_dispatch`; the workflow also runs on a `*/20`
  cron) and confirm the run's reconciler writes at least one **signed** event authored by
  identity `594c`, with `rebar verify-identity` reporting it `verified`.
- If a given run has no inbound Jira delta to translate (so it writes no work-ticket event),
  re-dispatch until a run produces a bot-authored ticket event, or use the workflow's own canary
  write — the AC is satisfied by a real signed event id, not merely a green run.

## Scope

The GitHub Actions workflows that write tickets (`reconcile-bridge` + its canary). Secret
population + a live signing proof only. No enforcement flip here (8d39); no change to the
workflow YAML (the wiring already exists — this story does not modify
`.github/workflows/reconcile-bridge.yml`). Empty `file_impact` is correct: no tracked repo file
changes.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **Secret populated from SSM.** `REBAR_BOT_SIGNING_KEY` on `navapbc/rebar` holds the Rebar
      Bot private key whose public half is registered on identity `594c`. Proving evidence
      recorded on this ticket: the `gh secret set` outcome + `gh secret list` line showing the
      updated timestamp. (Value is write-only, so proof is the successful set + the downstream
      signed event, not a read-back.)
- [ ] **A CI run produces a signed, verified bot event.** A `reconcile-bridge` run writes ≥1
      event authored by `594c`. Proving evidence: the work ticket `<t>` the run wrote shows
      `rebar show <t> | jq -e '.authorship.signed >= 1'` (signature present), and the new event
      **verifies** — record its event uuid `E` and assert its **absence** from the non-verified
      report (verified events are excluded — `verify_authorship.py:503-504`):
      `rebar verify-identity --all --format json | jq --arg u E -e 'all(.[]; .event_uuid != $u)'`
      exits 0. The event uuid `E` is recorded on this ticket.
- [ ] [operator-attested] The secret is set in GitHub settings and a real run proves signing.
      Evidence recorded on this ticket as a comment: the workflow-run URL, the `gh secret list`
      timestamp, and the resulting signed event id (+ its `verified` verdict from the command
      above).
